title: stenanthemum pumilum
intro: Stenanthemum pumilum, also known as the small button bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Stenanthemum pumilum can be made into teas, infusions, or dried for later use, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
